items.title: Customer churn and plan metadata
items.description: Telecom and SaaS models one-hot encode nominal plan and channel fields while ordinally encoding tenure bands or satisfaction tiers before logistic regression or gradient boosting.

items.title: E-commerce catalog attributes
items.description: Product category trees with thousands of leaf nodes use smoothed target encoding of historical conversion rate per category instead of sparse one-hot matrices.

items.title: Credit and fraud tabular features
items.description: Merchant category codes and device types are encoded for tree ensembles; rare levels are grouped or target-encoded with cross-validation to limit overfitting on thin segments.

items.title: Healthcare survey responses
items.description: Likert scales (strongly disagree → strongly agree) use ordinal integers; free-text departments use one-hot or hashed encodings depending on cardinality and privacy constraints.
